title: Phlomoides laciniata
taxonomy: Phlomoides laciniata is classified as a species within the genus Phlomoides, which belongs to the family Lamiaceae, commonly known as the mint family. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and its evolutionary relationships with other species. The genus Phlomoides is a group of flowering plants that are native to the mountainous regions of Asia and Europe. These plants are known for their unique flowers and foliage, and are often used in landscaping and horticulture. The classification of Phlomoides laciniata is a subject of ongoing research and study in the fields of botany and taxonomy.
